records.ety: [OE. kuggel; cf. G. keule club (with a round end), kugel ball, or perh. W. cogyl cudgel, or D. cudse, kuds, cudgel.]

defs: A staff used in cudgel play, shorter than the quarterstaff, and wielded with one hand; hence, any heavy stick used as a weapon.
quotes: He getteth him a grievous crabtree cudgel and . . . falls to rating of them as if they were dogs.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cudgeled or Cudgelled (-<?/ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Cudgeling or cudgelling.]

quotes: An he here, I would cudgel him like a dog.
